1→/** 2→ * formation-tutorial.js 3→ * 4→ * Tutorial system for teaching users about different merge formations 5→ * Shows a dialog when user creates a specific formation type for the first time 6→ */ 7→ 8→import { loadShownFormationTutorials, saveShownFormationTutorial } from "./storage.js"; 9→import { isBlocked, isBlockedWithLife, isBlockedMovable, isBlockedWithMergeCount } from "./tile-helpers.js"; 10→ 11→/** 12→ * Get pending formation tutorials for match groups 13→ * @param {Array} matchGroups - Array of match groups from findMatches() 14→ * @param {object|null} swapPosition - Last swap position {row, col, movedFrom: {row, col}} 15→ * @returns {Array<{formationType: string, matchGroup: object}>} Array of pending tutorials with their match groups 16→ */ 17→export function getPendingFormationTutorials(matchGroups, swapPosition = null) { 18→ const pending = []; 19→ const seenTypes = new Set(); 20→ const shownTutorials = loadShownFormationTutorials(); 21→ 22→ // Filter to only active merges (containing swapped tiles) if swapPosition provided 23→ const filteredGroups = swapPosition 24→ ? matchGroups.filter((group) => { 25→ return group.tiles.some( 26→ (tile) => 27→ (tile.row === swapPosition.row && tile.col === swapPosition.col) || 28→ (swapPosition.movedFrom && 29→ tile.row === swapPosition.movedFrom.row && 30→ tile.col === swapPosition.movedFrom.col) 31→ ); 32→ }) 33→ : matchGroups; 34→ 35→ filteredGroups.forEach((group) => { 36→ const formationType = getFormationTypeFromDirection(group.direction); 37→ if (formationType && !seenTypes.has(formationType) && !shownTutorials.has(formationType)) { 38→ seenTypes.add(formationType); 39→ pending.push({ formationType, matchGroup: group }); 40→ } 41→ }); 42→ 43→ return pending; 44→} 45→ 46→/** 47→ * Highlight tiles that will be merged using merge-preview class 48→ * @param {Array} matchGroups - Array of match groups to highlight 49→ */ 50→export function highlightMergeTiles(matchGroups) { 51→ matchGroups.forEach((group) => { 52→ group.tiles.forEach((tile) => { 53→ const gem = document.querySelector(`[data-row="${tile.row}"][data-col="${tile.col}"]`); 54→ if (gem) { 55→ gem.classList.add("merge-preview"); 56→ } 57→ }); 58→ }); 59→} 60→ 61→/** 62→ * Highlight blocked tiles adjacent to match tiles using unblock-preview class 63→ * @param {Match3Game} game - The game instance 64→ * @param {Array} matchGroups - Array of match groups 65→ */ 66→export function highlightBlockedTiles(game, matchGroups) { 67→ const highlighted = new Set(); 68→ 69→ matchGroups.forEach((group) => { 70→ group.tiles.forEach((tile) => { 71→ const adjacentPositions = [ 72→ { row: tile.row - 1, col: tile.col }, 73→ { row: tile.row + 1, col: tile.col }, 74→ { row: tile.row, col: tile.col - 1 }, 75→ { row: tile.row, col: tile.col + 1 }, 76→ ]; 77→ 78→ adjacentPositions.forEach((pos) => { 79→ if (pos.row >= 0 && pos.row < game.boardHeight && pos.col >= 0 && pos.col < game.boardWidth) { 80→ const key = `${pos.row}_${pos.col}`; 81→ if (highlighted.has(key)) return; 82→ 83→ const adjacentTile = game.board[pos.row]?.[pos.col]; 84→ if (adjacentTile && (isBlocked(adjacentTile) || isBlockedWithLife(adjacentTile) || isBlockedMovable(adjacentTile) || isBlockedWithMergeCount(adjacentTile))) { 85→ highlighted.add(key); 86→ const gem = document.querySelector(`[data-row="${pos.row}"][data-col="${pos.col}"]`); 87→ if (gem) { 88→ gem.classList.add("unblock-preview"); 89→ } 90→ } 91→ } 92→ }); 93→ }); 94→ }); 95→} 96→ 97→/** 98→ * Remove merge-preview and unblock-preview highlight from all tiles 99→ */ 100→export function clearMergeHighlight() { 101→ document.querySelectorAll(".gem.merge-preview").forEach((gem) => { 102→ gem.classList.remove("merge-preview"); 103→ }); 104→ document.querySelectorAll(".gem.unblock-preview").forEach((gem) => { 105→ gem.classList.remove("unblock-preview"); 106→ }); 107→} 108→ 109→// Dialog content for each formation type 110→export const FORMATION_TUTORIAL_DIALOGS = { 111→ line_3: { 112→ subtitle: "3-Tile Match", 113→ title: "Line", 114→ content: ` 115→

Here it is: --- ## Source File Overview ### NEW Files **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/formation-tutorial.js`** Shows one-time dialogs when a player creates a new formation type for the first time. Exports: - `getPendingFormationTutorials(matchGroups, swapPosition)` - filters which tutorials need showing - `highlightMergeTiles(matchGroups)` / `highlightBlockedTiles(game, matchGroups)` / `clearMergeHighlight()` - DOM preview highlighting for drag previews - `showFormationTutorialDialog(formationType)` - creates and shows the overlay dialog (returns Promise) - `FORMATION_TUTORIAL_DIALOGS` - inline HTML content for each of: line_3, line_4, block_4, line_5, t_formation, l_formation - `getFormationTypeFromDirection(direction)` - maps internal direction strings to dialog keys --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/goal-dialogs.js`** One-time intro dialogs for new goal types and feature unlock dialogs. Exports: - `showGoalDialog(dialogType, game, onClose)` - shows a goal-type intro dialog - `showGoalDialogsSequence(dialogTypes, game, onComplete)` - shows multiple in series - `showFeatureUnlockDialog(featureKey, game, onClose)` - "X Unlocked!" dialogs for streaks, board upgrades, power-ups - `getDialogsToShow(levelGoals)` - returns dialog types not yet shown for this level - `hasDialogBeenShown(dialogType)`, `hasFeatureBeenUnlocked(featureKey)` - `updateIntroDialogPowerupsList(game)` - populates the intro dialog's power-up list - `GOAL_TYPE_DESCRIPTIONS`, `FEATURE_DESCRIPTIONS`, `GOAL_DIALOGS`, `FEATURE_UNLOCK_DIALOGS` - content constants --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/gravity.js`** Pure headless gravity logic, no DOM or animation. Extracted so both `animator.js` and `simulation.js` can share it. Single export: - `applyGravity(game)` - mutates `game.board` in-place: drops tiles down, handles rectangular blocked tiles as section dividers, spawns new tiles in top sections. Returns `{ movements, newTiles }`. --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/hint-system.js`** Finds and scores the best possible swap for the hint feature. Exports: - `findBestSwap(game)` - evaluates all valid adjacent swaps by temporarily mutating board copies, scores them by formation type, tile value, goal progress, blocked-tile clearing, and position. Returns best swap with direction info and matching tile positions. - `getMatchTilesForSwap(game, row1, col1, row2, col2)` - used during drag preview to show which tiles would match (pre-swap coordinates). Scoring priorities (highest to lowest): blocked tile clearing (10000+ pts), formation type (line_5=1000, T/L=800, line_4=600, block_4=500, line_3=300), goal progress, tile count, special tile creation, position bonus. --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/home-screen.js`** Manages the home screen between levels (hearts, coins, streaks, shop). Exports: - `showHomeScreen(game)` - shows home screen with updated displays; checks/triggers feature unlock dialogs - `hideHomeScreen()` - switches back to game container - `initializeHomeScreen(game)` - sets up all event listeners (level button, no-hearts dialog, shop, coin purchases) - `updateHeartsDisplay(game, el)` - renders hearts + countdown timer with auto-refresh interval Internal: `updateSuperStreakDisplay` (SVG circular progress bar), `updateStreakDisplay` (3-section progress bar), `updateCoinsDisplay`, `checkAndShowFeatureUnlockDialogs`. --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/power-ups.js`** All power-up logic extracted from `game.js`. Exports: - `setupPowerUps(game)` - attaches click handlers to power-up buttons - `activatePowerUp(game, type)` / `deactivatePowerUp(game)` - `handlePowerUpAction(game, row, col, element)` - dispatches to hammer/halve/wildcard handlers - `usePowerUpHammer(game, row, col, el)` - removes tiles (handles normal, blocked, blocked_with_life, blocked_with_merge_count, rectangular variants) - `usePowerUpHalve(game, row, col, el)` - halves tile value (works on cursed tiles too) - `usePowerUpWildcard(game, row, col, el)` - converts normal tile to joker - `grantRandomPowerUp(game)` / `grantPowerUp(game, type)` / `consumePowerUp(game, type)` - `grantFormationPowerUp(game, formationType)` - grants specific power-up for L/T/line_5 formations - `getTotalPowerUpCount(game, type)` - sums persistent + transient counts - `isPowerUpButtonVisible(game, type)` / `getVisiblePowerUpTypes(game)` - slot unlock gating - `updatePowerUpButtons(game)` / `updatePowerUpCycleIndicator(game)` - `showPowerUpRewardAnimation(game, type)` - floating "+1 🔨" animation using `` - `showPowerUps(game)` / `hidePowerUps(game)` - `setupPowerupShop(game)` / `openPowerupShop(game)` - in-game power-up purchase dialog --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/serializer.js`** Board serialization/deserialization for logging, storage, and level presets. Exports: - `parsePresetTile(notation)` - parses tile notation strings: `B`, `BM`, `B64`, `J`, `JT`, `2S`, `2K`, `2P`, `2C5`, or plain numbers - `tileToNotation(tile)` - inverse of above - `serializeBoard(board)` - 2D array → `"2,4,B|J,2S,4"` (rows `|` separated, tiles `,` separated) - `deserializeBoard(serialized)` - inverse - `parsePresetArray(preset)` - converts `config.js` array presets to tile objects --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/settings-dialog.js`** Settings and info dialog setup extracted from `game.js`. Exports: - `setupSettingsButton(game)` - wires the settings dialog (level select, level config set, board upgrade action, special tile rewards per formation, hints, non-matching swaps, power-up cycle, persistent power-ups, power-up selection). Saves all via `storage.js`. Reloads page on level change. - `setupInfoButton(game)` - wires the info/intro dialog and reset button. --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/simulation.js`** Headless game simulation for move evaluation (used by the hint system). Single export: - `simulateMove(game, { row1, col1, row2, col2 })` - deep-clones board, runs the full match→merge→gravity cascade loop without any DOM/animation. Returns `{ valid, newBoard, scoreDelta, goalProgress, cascades }`. Creates a minimal `simContext` proxy that satisfies the interfaces expected by `findMatches`, `processMerges`, and `applyGravity`. --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/tracker.js`** Analytics event tracking (no-op in development). Exports: - `track(eventName, parameters, options)` - POSTs to `octolytics.lotum.com/v3/collect`; skipped on localhost/IP - `trackLevelSolved(game)` - fires `level_solved` with moves, duration, power-up usage, match counts, settings, board state - `trackLevelLost(game)` - fires `level_lost` with similar data plus goals remaining - `cyrb53(str, seed)` - fast 53-bit string hash (used for user ID hashing in settings display) - `isDevelopment()` - returns true for localhost/IP hosts --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/tutorial.js`** FTUX (First Time User Experience) step-by-step tutorial system driven by `tutorialSwaps` in level configs. Exports: - `initTutorial(game)` - reads `levelConfig.tutorialSwaps`, initializes `game.tutorialState` - `isTutorialActive(game)`, `getCurrentTutorialSwap(game)` - `isValidTutorialSwap(game, r1, c1, r2, c2)` - bidirectional check - `isTutorialTapStep(game)`, `isValidTutorialTap(game, row, col)` - `isTutorialPowerUpStep(game)`, `isValidTutorialPowerUp(game, type)`, `isValidTutorialPowerUpTarget(game, row, col)` - `canDragTileInTutorial(game, row, col)` - restricts which tiles can be touched - `advanceTutorialStep(game)` / `completeTutorial(game)` - `showTutorialUI(game)` / `updateTutorialUI(game)` / `hideTutorialUI(game)` - manage animated hand overlay and hint text; internally call `updateTutorialSwapAnimation`, `updateTutorialTapAnimation`, `updateTutorialPowerUpAnimation` Each tutorial step is `{ from, to, text }` (swap) or `{ tap, text }` (tap) or `{ powerUp: { type, target }, text }` (power-up). --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/version.js`** Single constant: `export const APP_VERSION = "1.0.0";` --- **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/components/stroked-text.js`** Custom Web Component `` that renders text with an SVG stroke outline. Attributes: `text`, `font-size`, `fill`, `stroke`, `stroke-width`, `width`, `height`, `font-weight`, `text-anchor`, `svg-style`. For emoji text, falls back to an offscreen canvas approach that draws the emoji with a colored stroke ring, then embeds as a data URL ``. Registered as `customElements.define("stroked-text", StrokedText)`. Note: it lives at `/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/src/components/stroked-text.js` (not `src/components/`). --- ### CHANGED Existing Files (responsibility shifts) **`game.js`** - Still the central orchestrator/`Match3Game` class, but significantly slimmed down. Power-up logic moved to `power-ups.js`, settings dialog setup to `settings-dialog.js`. Now imports from `tracker.js`, `hint-system.js`, `home-screen.js`, `power-ups.js`, `settings-dialog.js`, `tutorial.js`, `goal-dialogs.js`, `formation-tutorial.js`. Game state additions: `hearts`, `coins`, `currentStreak`, `superStreak`, `hintsEnabled`, `hintTimeout`, `allowNonMatchingSwaps`, `formationPowerUpRewards`, `persistentPowerUpsEnabled`, `powerUpCounts` (now with `persistent`/`transient` split), `selectedPowerUps`, `tutorialState`, `matchStats`, `levelStartTime`. **`animator.js`** - Now imports `applyGravity` from `gravity.js` instead of containing gravity logic itself. Also imports `trySwap` from `input-handler.js`. Core animation functions unchanged. **`main.js`** - Now does much more than before: registers/unregisters service worker, checks `APP_VERSION` for major-version resets, initializes home screen via `initializeHomeScreen(game)`, shows home screen on load, adds keyboard shortcuts (n/b for level navigation). No longer injects dynamic CSS. **`goal-tracker.js`** - Expanded: now handles cursed tile timers (`decrementCursedTileTimers`), integrates with `showHomeScreen`, imports `findBestSwap` for post-level-completion hint resets. Added streak/super-streak save logic on level complete. **`storage.js`** - Significantly expanded with new keys: `loadStreak`/`saveStreak`, `loadSuperStreak`/`saveSuperStreak`, `loadHearts`/`saveHearts`, `loadLastRegenTime`/`saveLastRegenTime`, `loadCoins`/`saveCoins`, `loadPowerUpCounts`/`savePowerUpCounts`, `loadShownGoalDialogs`/`saveShownGoalDialog`, `loadShownFormationTutorials`/`saveShownFormationTutorial`, `isFeatureUnlocked`/`saveUnlockedFeature`, `loadUserId`/`generateUUID`, `loadVersion`/`saveVersion`, `loadHintsEnabled`, `loadHintTimeoutMs`, `loadAllowNonMatchingSwaps`, `loadFormationPowerUpRewards`, `loadPersistentPowerUpsEnabled`, `loadSelectedPowerUps`, and others. **`renderer.js`** - Now handles many more tile types in DOM rendering: `freeswap_horizontal`, `freeswap_vertical`, `hammer`, `halver`, `teleport`, `wild_teleport`, `plus`, rectangular blocked tiles (using CSS Grid `grid-area`). Added `renderBoardUpgrades`, `renderPowerUpRewards`, `renderHintHighlight`. Now imports `isFeatureUnlocked` and uses `SUPER_STREAK_THRESHOLD` for board upgrade display. **`config.js`** - Added `FEATURE_KEYS` export (`POWER_UP_1`, `POWER_UP_2`, `POWER_UP_3`, `STREAK`, `BOARD_UPGRADES`, `SUPER_STREAK`). Added new `SPECIAL_TILE_TYPES`: `STICKY_FREESWAP`, `FREESWAP_HORIZONTAL`, `FREESWAP_VERTICAL`, `HAMMER`, `HALVER`, `RANDOM_POWERUP`, `TELEPORT`, `WILD_TELEPORT`, `PLUS`. Added `SUPER_STREAK_THRESHOLD`. Added new `TILE_TYPE.CURSED`. Multiple `LEVEL_CONFIGS` sets (keyed arrays with a `key` field) replacing single level list. `DEFAULT_LEVEL` export for fallback. **`tile-helpers.js`** - Many new tile creator functions: `createBlockedWithLifeTile`, `createCursedTile`, `createWildTeleportTile`. New type checks: `isBlockedMovable`, `isBlockedWithMergeCount`, `isCursed`, `isRectangularBlocked`, `isWildTeleportTile`, `isTileFreeSwapHorizontalTile`, `isTileFreeSwapVerticalTile`, `isTileHammerTile`, `isTileHalverTile`, `isTileTeleportTile`, `isTilePlusTile`, `isTileStickyFreeSwapTile`. Added `findBestJokerValue`, `getFontSize`. **`match-detector.js`**, **`merge-processor.js`**, **`board.js`**, **`input-handler.js`** - Core logic mostly stable but adapted for new tile types (cursed, wild_teleport, plus, rectangular blocked) and new game state fields. --- ### Other Files **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/styles/game.css`** - Game screen styles (formerly `style.css`). Styles `.game-container`, board, tiles, animations, dialogs. **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/styles/home.css`** - Home screen styles: hearts, coins, streak bars, super-streak SVG circle, shop dialog. **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/style.css`** - The original root CSS file (still present, possibly the entry point that imports or duplicates styles). **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/sw.js`** - Service worker. Cache name `match-2048-v0.4.0`. Pre-caches `index.html`, `style.css`, `src/main.js`. On activate: deletes old caches, claims clients, sends `SW_UPDATED` message to trigger page reload. **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/manifest.json`** - PWA manifest. `display: "standalone"`, portrait orientation, `#303030` theme, single 512x512 `assets/logo.png` icon. **`/Users/garreltmock/Projects/match-2048/levelBuilder/index.js`** - Node.js CLI script (`#!/usr/bin/env node`). `LevelBuilder` class for analyzing, generating, and balancing level configs. Uses difficulty scoring based on board size, move count, goal difficulty. Not part of the browser bundle - a developer tool only.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Match 2048 is a browser-based puzzle game that combines match-3 mechanics with 2048-style tile merging. Players swap adjacent tiles to create matches of 3+ identical numbers, which then merge into higher-value tiles. Each level has specific tile goals that must be achieved within a limited number of moves. The game features power-ups, blocked tiles, and configurable board sizes for increased challenge and strategy.

## Architecture

This is a vanilla JavaScript single-page application using ES6 modules with no build process or dependencies:

- `index.html` - Main HTML structure with game UI and tutorial overlay
- `style.css` - Complete styling with responsive design and animations
- `src/` - Modular JavaScript source files using ES6 modules

### Module Structure

The game is organized into focused, single-responsibility modules:

**`config.js`** - Game constants and level definitions
- `TILE_TYPE`, `SPECIAL_TILE_TYPES`, `FORMATION_TYPES` constants
- All 19 level configurations with goals, board sizes, blocked tiles, and spawnable tiles
- Default tile values and power-up limits

**`storage.js`** - LocalStorage persistence
- Load/save functions for: current level, score, numberBase, showReviewBoard, specialTileConfig
- All keys prefixed with "match2048_"

**`tile-helpers.js`** - Tile object utilities
- Tile creation functions: `createTile()`, `createBlockedTile()`, `createJokerTile()`
- Type checking: `isBlocked()`, `isJoker()`, `isNormal()`, `isTilePowerTile()`, etc.
- Value display conversion with `getDisplayValue()`

**`board.js`** - Board state and generation
- `createBoard()` - Generates initial board without matches, places blocked tiles
- `getRandomTileValue()` - Random tile selection from spawnable values
- `canMatch()` - Power tile matching logic

**`input-handler.js`** - User input processing
- Touch and mouse event handlers
- Drag-to-swap mechanics with preview
- Joker activation by tap
- Free swap tile handling

**`match-detector.js`** - Match finding logic
- `findMatches()` - Main matching algorithm with priority system
- Special formation detection: `checkTFormation()`, `checkLFormation()`, `checkBlockFormation()`
- Joker tile integration during user swaps
- Match priority: 5-line > T > L > 4-line > Block > 3-line

**`merge-processor.js`** - Match processing and merging
- `processMatches()` - Entry point for match processing
- `createMergedTiles()` - Creates merged tiles with special tile types
- `determineSpecialTilePosition()` - Smart positioning based on swap location
- `unblockAdjacentTiles()` - Removes blocked tiles near matches

**`animator.js`** - Animation coordination
- `animateSwap()` / `animateRevert()` - Tile swap animations
- `animateMerges()` - Match merge animations
- `dropGems()` - Gravity physics with cascading matches
- `animateUnblocking()` - Blocked tile removal animations

**`renderer.js`** - DOM rendering
- `renderBoard()` - Creates DOM elements for all tiles with proper classes
- `renderGoals()` - Goal cards with progress indicators
- `updateMovesDisplay()`, `updateGoalDisplay()` - UI updates

**`goal-tracker.js`** - Goal and level progression
- `checkLevelComplete()` - Victory/game over detection
- `updateTileCounts()` - Tracks tiles on board for "current" goals
- `updateBlockedTileGoals()` - Tracks blocked tile clearing progress
- `nextLevel()`, `restartLevel()` - Level navigation

**`game.js`** - Main Match3Game class
- Orchestrates all modules as the central controller
- Manages game state (board, score, moves, power-ups, etc.)
- UI setup methods (dialogs, settings, power-ups)
- Delegates to module functions while maintaining state

**`main.js`** - Entry point
- Initializes game on DOMContentLoaded
- Injects dynamic CSS styles

### Core Game Systems

**Board Management** (`board.js`): Configurable board sizes (6x6 to 8x8) using 2D array, prevents initial matches during board generation, supports blocked tiles

**Match Detection** (`match-detector.js`): Hierarchical matching system prioritizing special formations (T, L, block) over regular 3+ matches

**Animation System** (`animator.js`): Coordinated animations for swaps, merges, and tile drops using CSS transitions and timeouts

**Level Progression** (`config.js`): 19 predefined levels with configurable board sizes, blocked tile patterns, spawnable tiles, and specific tile goals

**Power-up System** (`game.js`): Three power-ups with limited uses per level (hammer, halve, swap) providing strategic advantages

**Blocked Tiles** (`board.js`, `merge-processor.js`): Immovable tiles that create obstacles and are cleared by adjacent matches

**Persistence** (`storage.js`): Uses localStorage for current level, score, and user preferences

### Game Flow

1. **Input Handling** (`input-handler.js`): Touch and mouse drag-to-swap mechanics, power-up activation system
2. **Match Processing** (`match-detector.js`): Complex formation detection with priority system
3. **Tile Merging** (`merge-processor.js`): Different merge rules for formations (T/L → 4x value, Block → 2 tiles at 2x, Regular → 2x value)
4. **Board Physics** (`animator.js`): Gravity-based tile dropping with new tile generation, respects blocked tiles
5. **Power-up Effects** (`game.js`): Strategic tile removal, value halving, and position swapping
6. **Goal Checking** (`goal-tracker.js`): Real-time progress tracking with completion detection based on tiles created (not current board state)

## Key Implementation Details

- **Tile Values**: Powers of 2 from 2 to 2048+ (or powers of 3 in settings)
- **Board Sizes**: Configurable from 6x6 to 10x10 depending on level design
- **Special Formations**: T and L shapes (5 tiles → 1 tile at 4x), Block shapes (4 tiles → 2 tiles at 2x)
- **Blocked Tiles**: Immovable obstacles defined per level with row/column-based positioning
- **Power-ups**: Three types with 2 uses each per level - Hammer (🔨 remove tile), Halver (✂️ halve value), Swap (🔄 swap any two tiles)
- **Special Tiles**: Configurable rewards for formations - Joker, Power (bonus matches), Golden (+1 value), Free Swap
- **Spawnable Tiles**: Level-configurable tile values that can appear during gameplay
- **Goal Types**: "created" (cumulative tiles made), "current" (tiles on board), "blocked" (blocked tiles cleared)
- **Animation States**: Uses CSS classes for different animation types (falling, sliding, merge-target, etc.)
- **Responsive Design**: Extensive use of `clamp()` for scalable UI across devices
- **Touch Support**: Full touch/swipe gesture support with preventDefault for mobile

## Code Conventions

- ES6 modules with explicit imports/exports
- Class-based architecture in `game.js`, functional modules elsewhere
- Extensive use of CSS Grid and Flexbox
- Animation timing coordinated with setTimeout/CSS transitions
- localStorage keys prefixed with "match2048_"
- DOM manipulation using vanilla JavaScript APIs
- Module functions receive `game` instance as parameter for state access

## User Notes

- Do not start any server
- Do not call "open index.html"
- I will test everything manually
- You do not have to add :hover and :active css modifier, since this is a mobile-first game

## Project Overview

Players swap adjacent tiles to create matches of 3+ identical numbers, merging into higher-value tiles. Each level has specific tile goals within limited moves. Features include power-ups, blocked/cursed tiles, configurable board sizes, a hint system, streaks, hearts/coins economy, and a home screen between levels. PWA-enabled with service worker.

## Architecture

- `index.html` - Main HTML structure with game UI, home screen, and dialogs
- `style.css` - Root CSS entry point

- `src/` - Modular JavaScript source files
- `sw.js` - Service worker (cache `match-2048-v0.4.0`)
- `manifest.json` - PWA manifest (standalone, portrait)
- `levelBuilder/` - Node.js CLI tool for analyzing/generating level configs (dev only)
- `assets/` - Static assets (logo, etc.)

### Module Structure

#### Core Game Logic

**`config.js`** - Game constants and level definitions
- `TILE_TYPE`, `SPECIAL_TILE_TYPES`, `FORMATION_TYPES`, `FEATURE_KEYS` constants
- Multiple `LEVEL_CONFIGS` sets (keyed arrays), `DEFAULT_LEVEL`, `SUPER_STREAK_THRESHOLD`
- Special tile types: joker, power, golden, free_swap, freeswap_horizontal/vertical, hammer, halver, teleport, wild_teleport, plus, sticky_freeswap, random_powerup

**`board.js`** - Board state and generation
- `createBoard()` - Generates initial board without matches, places blocked tiles
- `getRandomTileValue()` - Random tile from spawnable values
- `canMatch()` - Power tile matching logic

**`tile-helpers.js`** - Tile object utilities

- `getDisplayValue()`, `findBestJokerValue()`, `getFontSize()`

**`match-detector.js`** - Match finding logic

- Formation detection: `checkTFormation()`, `checkLFormation()`, `checkBlockFormation()`
- Supports new tile types (cursed, wild_teleport, plus)

**`merge-processor.js`** - Match processing and merging
- `processMatches()`, `createMergedTiles()`, `determineSpecialTilePosition()`
- `unblockAdjacentTiles()` - Clears blocked tiles near matches
- Handles all tile type variants

**`gravity.js`** - Pure headless gravity logic (no DOM)

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Match 2048 is a browser-based puzzle game that combines match-3 mechanics with 2048-style tile merging. Players swap adjacent tiles to create matches of 3+ identical numbers, which then merge into higher-value tiles. Each level has specific tile goals that must be achieved within a limited number of moves. The game features power-ups, blocked tiles, and configurable board sizes for increased challenge and strategy.

## Architecture

This is a vanilla JavaScript single-page application using ES6 modules with no build process or dependencies:

- `index.html` - Main HTML structure with game UI and tutorial overlay
- `style.css` - Complete styling with responsive design and animations
- `src/` - Modular JavaScript source files using ES6 modules

### Module Structure

The game is organized into focused, single-responsibility modules:

**`config.js`** - Game constants and level definitions
- `TILE_TYPE`, `SPECIAL_TILE_TYPES`, `FORMATION_TYPES` constants
- All 19 level configurations with goals, board sizes, blocked tiles, and spawnable tiles
- Default tile values and power-up limits

**`storage.js`** - LocalStorage persistence
- Load/save functions for: current level, score, numberBase, showReviewBoard, specialTileConfig
- All keys prefixed with "match2048_"

**`tile-helpers.js`** - Tile object utilities
- Tile creation functions: `createTile()`, `createBlockedTile()`, `createJokerTile()`
- Type checking: `isBlocked()`, `isJoker()`, `isNormal()`, `isTilePowerTile()`, etc.
- Value display conversion with `getDisplayValue()`

**`board.js`** - Board state and generation
- `createBoard()` - Generates initial board without matches, places blocked tiles
- `getRandomTileValue()` - Random tile selection from spawnable values
- `canMatch()` - Power tile matching logic

**`input-handler.js`** - User input processing
- Touch and mouse event handlers
- Drag-to-swap mechanics with preview
- Joker activation by tap
- Free swap tile handling

**`match-detector.js`** - Match finding logic
- `findMatches()` - Main matching algorithm with priority system
- Special formation detection: `checkTFormation()`, `checkLFormation()`, `checkBlockFormation()`
- Joker tile integration during user swaps
- Match priority: 5-line > T > L > 4-line > Block > 3-line

**`merge-processor.js`** - Match processing and merging
- `processMatches()` - Entry point for match processing
- `createMergedTiles()` - Creates merged tiles with special tile types
- `determineSpecialTilePosition()` - Smart positioning based on swap location
- `unblockAdjacentTiles()` - Removes blocked tiles near matches

**`animator.js`** - Animation coordination
- `animateSwap()` / `animateRevert()` - Tile swap animations
- `animateMerges()` - Match merge animations
- `dropGems()` - Gravity physics with cascading matches
- `animateUnblocking()` - Blocked tile removal animations

**`renderer.js`** - DOM rendering
- `renderBoard()` - Creates DOM elements for all tiles with proper classes
- `renderGoals()` - Goal cards with progress indicators
- `updateMovesDisplay()`, `updateGoalDisplay()` - UI updates

**`goal-tracker.js`** - Goal and level progression
- `checkLevelComplete()` - Victory/game over detection
- `updateTileCounts()` - Tracks tiles on board for "current" goals
- `updateBlockedTileGoals()` - Tracks blocked tile clearing progress
- `nextLevel()`, `restartLevel()` - Level navigation

**`game.js`** - Main Match3Game class
- Orchestrates all modules as the central controller
- Manages game state (board, score, moves, power-ups, etc.)
- UI setup methods (dialogs, settings, power-ups)
- Delegates to module functions while maintaining state

**`main.js`** - Entry point
- Initializes game on DOMContentLoaded
- Injects dynamic CSS styles

### Core Game Systems

**Board Management** (`board.js`): Configurable board sizes (6x6 to 8x8) using 2D array, prevents initial matches during board generation, supports blocked tiles

**Match Detection** (`match-detector.js`): Hierarchical matching system prioritizing special formations (T, L, block) over regular 3+ matches

**Animation System** (`animator.js`): Coordinated animations for swaps, merges, and tile drops using CSS transitions and timeouts

**Level Progression** (`config.js`): 19 predefined levels with configurable board sizes, blocked tile patterns, spawnable tiles, and specific tile goals

**Power-up System** (`game.js`): Three power-ups with limited uses per level (hammer, halve, swap) providing strategic advantages

**Blocked Tiles** (`board.js`, `merge-processor.js`): Immovable tiles that create obstacles and are cleared by adjacent matches

**Persistence** (`storage.js`): Uses localStorage for current level, score, and user preferences

### Game Flow

1. **Input Handling** (`input-handler.js`): Touch and mouse drag-to-swap mechanics, power-up activation system
2. **Match Processing** (`match-detector.js`): Complex formation detection with priority system
3. **Tile Merging** (`merge-processor.js`): Different merge rules for formations (T/L → 4x value, Block → 2 tiles at 2x, Regular → 2x value)
4. **Board Physics** (`animator.js`): Gravity-based tile dropping with new tile generation, respects blocked tiles
5. **Power-up Effects** (`game.js`): Strategic tile removal, value halving, and position swapping
6. **Goal Checking** (`goal-tracker.js`): Real-time progress tracking with completion detection based on tiles created (not current board state)

## Key Implementation Details

- **Tile Values**: Powers of 2 from 2 to 2048+ (or powers of 3 in settings)
- **Board Sizes**: Configurable from 6x6 to 10x10 depending on level design
- **Special Formations**: T and L shapes (5 tiles → 1 tile at 4x), Block shapes (4 tiles → 2 tiles at 2x)
- **Blocked Tiles**: Immovable obstacles defined per level with row/column-based positioning
- **Power-ups**: Three types with 2 uses each per level - Hammer (🔨 remove tile), Halver (✂️ halve value), Swap (🔄 swap any two tiles)
- **Special Tiles**: Configurable rewards for formations - Joker, Power (bonus matches), Golden (+1 value), Free Swap
- **Spawnable Tiles**: Level-configurable tile values that can appear during gameplay
- **Goal Types**: "created" (cumulative tiles made), "current" (tiles on board), "blocked" (blocked tiles cleared)
- **Animation States**: Uses CSS classes for different animation types (falling, sliding, merge-target, etc.)
- **Responsive Design**: Extensive use of `clamp()` for scalable UI across devices
- **Touch Support**: Full touch/swipe gesture support with preventDefault for mobile

## Code Conventions

- ES6 modules with explicit imports/exports
- Class-based architecture in `game.js`, functional modules elsewhere
- Extensive use of CSS Grid and Flexbox
- Animation timing coordinated with setTimeout/CSS transitions
- localStorage keys prefixed with "match2048_"
- DOM manipulation using vanilla JavaScript APIs
- Module functions receive `game` instance as parameter for state access

## User Notes

- Do not start any server
- Do not call "open index.html"
- I will test everything manually
- You do not have to add :hover and :active css modifier, since this is a mobile-first game
